assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Each slice undergoes atomic recreation - not rolling updates like regular pods' and 'All pods on each slice terminate and recreate in a single step'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Any maintenance on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL slices' and 'All 4 slices (1,024 TPU v5e chips total) will restart simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Your entire training job will be interrupted' and 'All pods on each slice terminate and recreate in a single step'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response states 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions 'extended downtime beyond the normal maintenance window'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force a checkpoint save before the maintenance window' with specific kubectl commands and verification steps

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Test checkpoint resume in a dev environment' and includes 'Resume from latest checkpoint' command with specific kubectl example

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Don't assume maintenance exclusions help - GKE host maintenance bypasses cluster-level controls' which contradicts the expectation to suggest using maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Maintenance Window Planning' section that advises planning for downtime and coordinating with research team, though it doesn't explicitly mention controlling when the event occurs
